Terms of Service & Data Use Agreement

Welcome to Peak Athlete

Before using our services, please review and accept our Terms of Service and Data Use Agreement. By using Peak Athlete, you agree to these terms.

1. Acceptance of Terms

By accessing or using Peak Athlete services, you agree to be bound by these Terms of Service. If you do not agree to these terms, please do not use our services.

2. Service Description

Peak Athlete provides swimming performance analysis services, including video analysis, split times, stroke metrics, and related analytics. Our services are intended for educational and performance improvement purposes only.

3. User Accounts

  • You must provide accurate and complete information when creating an account
  • You are responsible for maintaining the security of your account credentials
  • Users of any age may use the service with parental or guardian consent if under 18
  • You agree to notify us immediately of any unauthorized access to your account

4. Data Collection & Use

By using Peak Athlete, you authorize us to collect and process:

  • Video/imagery (above-water and underwater)
  • Performance metrics (splits, start/turn metrics, stroke data, velocities)
  • Session metadata (date/time, event type, equipment settings)
  • Analysis notes and derived metrics

We use this data to: (i) deliver analysis services; (ii) create anonymized benchmarks and comparisons; (iii) improve our software and algorithms; (iv) generate de-identified statistics and insights.

5. Data Sharing

We may share your data with confidential, contract-bound service providers (cloud storage, video processing) and technology partners (including Contemplas) only as necessary to provide our services. Recipients may not use data for marketing, sell it, or disclose it except to perform their limited functions.

6. Payment Terms

  • All purchases are final and non-refundable
  • Subscription fees are billed in advance on a monthly basis
  • You may cancel your subscription at any time; access continues until the end of the billing period
  • Purchased analysis credits never expire

7. Service Limitations

  • Services are provided "as-is" without warranty of any kind
  • We do not guarantee uptime, accuracy, or specific performance outcomes
  • Our analysis is not medical advice and is not a substitute for professional coaching
  • We are not liable for training decisions made based on our analysis

8. Account Termination

Peak Athlete reserves the right to suspend or terminate accounts at our discretion. Upon termination, your data may be deleted. We recommend downloading any videos or data you wish to keep.

9. Intellectual Property

Peak Athlete retains ownership of all software, analysis methods, algorithms, benchmarks, and derived datasets. You retain ownership of your original video content.

10. Indemnification

You agree to indemnify and hold harmless Peak Athlete from any claims arising from your use of the service, violation of these terms, or infringement of any third-party rights.

11. Limitation of Liability

To the maximum extent permitted by law, Peak Athlete shall not be liable for any indirect, incidental, special, consequential, or punitive damages arising from your use of the service.

12. Governing Law

These terms are governed by the laws of the State of Florida, Broward County, without regard to conflict of law principles.

13. Changes to Terms

We may modify these terms at any time. Continued use of the service after changes constitutes acceptance of the new terms.

14. Contact

For questions about these terms, contact us at the email provided on our website.

Agreement Version 1.0 • Effective February 2026

Start Analysis

Dive start performance metrics and trends

Scope
Filters

Select Start Trial

Loading trials...
Click a trial to view details. Click a second to compare.
Loading start metrics...
Block Phase
Flight & Entry
Underwater
Velocity
Start Summary
Video

Select a start trial to view block phase details

Select a start trial to view flight and entry data

Select a start trial to view underwater phase

Split Times

Underwater Velocity Speed during the underwater phase. Aim to hold velocity above 1.8 m/s through breakout. A sharp drop means early surfacing or weak kicks.

Velocity Segments Full velocity profile from block to 15m. Should show a high peak at takeoff (4+ m/s) with a gradual taper. Sudden drops indicate energy loss at entry or transition.

Speed across 0-5m, 5-10m, and 10-15m zones

Select a start trial to view summary

This start trial does not have a linked video.

Your videos are stored forever

Turn Analysis

Wall turn performance, approach, and underwater metrics

Scope
Filters

Select Turn Trial

Loading trials...
Click a trial to view details. Click a second to compare.
Loading turn metrics...
Approach
Wall Contact
Underwater
Velocity Profile
Turn Summary
Video

Select a turn trial to view approach data

Approach Velocity Speed in the last 15m before the wall, split into three 5m zones. Watch for big drop-offs - maintain speed into the wall.

Select a turn trial to view wall contact data

Select a turn trial to view underwater phase

Post-Turn Velocity Velocity after the wall push-off. Good turns maintain 2.0+ m/s through the underwater before breakout.

Full Velocity Profile Complete velocity: approach (3 zones) -> wall -> departure (3 zones). Look for symmetry - approach speed should be matched or exceeded in departure.

Approach through departure - 6 velocity zones

Select a turn trial to view summary

This turn trial does not have a linked video.

Race Analysis

Race performance, splits, pacing, and stroke analysis

Scope
Filters

Select a Race

Click a race to view details. Click a second to compare.
Loading race metrics...
Split Analysis
Pacing
Stroke Data
Race Summary
Video

Split Times Cumulative time at each wall. Switch to "Per Segment" to see individual lap times and identify pacing patterns.

Lap Split Breakdown Visual comparison of each lap. Green = fastest, blue = average, red = slowest. Even splits indicate good pacing strategy.

Loading splits...

Velocity per Segment Average speed per segment. Watch for negative splits (getting faster) vs positive splits (slowing down). Elite pacing often shows a slight U-shape.

Start Phase Breakdown Breakdown of time in each phase of the race start: on-block, flight, and underwater to surface break.

Loading...

Stroke Rate Strokes per minute in each segment. Increasing stroke rate with decreasing distance-per-stroke can indicate fatigue.

Stroke Count per Lap Total strokes per lap. Fewer strokes with the same split time means better efficiency (distance per stroke).

Loading...
